questions.text: State the relative charge and relative mass of a proton.
Calculate the empirical formula of a compound containing 40% carbon, 6.7% hydrogen and 53.3% oxygen by mass.
questions.markScheme: Assume 100 g: C=40/12=3.33, H=6.7/1=6.7, O=53.3/16=3.33. Ratio 1:2:1. Empirical formula CH2O.

questions.text: Explain why cryolite is added during the electrolysis of aluminium oxide.
questions.markScheme: Lowers melting point of electrolyte, reduces energy needed, increases conductivity / lowers cost.

questions.text: Describe a test to identify hydrogen gas.
questions.markScheme: Bring a lit splint to the gas; hydrogen burns with a squeaky pop.

questions.text: State two factors that increase the rate of reaction between marble chips and hydrochloric acid.
questions.markScheme: Any two: increase temperature, increase acid concentration, use smaller chips (greater surface area), add catalyst.

questions.text: Name the functional group in ethanol and give one reaction characteristic of this group.
questions.markScheme: Functional group is hydroxyl (-OH). Example: oxidation to ethanoic acid / combustion to CO2 and H2O / reaction with sodium to produce hydrogen.

questions.text: Describe the test for chloride ions in aqueous solution.
questions.markScheme: Acidify with dilute nitric acid, add aqueous silver nitrate, white precipitate (silver chloride) forms.

questions.text: Why are alloys generally harder than pure metals?
questions.markScheme: Different sized atoms in alloys distort layers and prevent them sliding easily, making alloy harder.
